# **Implementation Plan & Testing Roadmap: Astrometric Space-Time Terminal**

**Project Name:** Astrometric Horizon Terminal (Interactive Relativistic Simulator)

**Document Reference:** PLAN.md

**Version:** 2.0

**Status:** Approved Architectural Staging

## **1\. Modular Architecture Blueprint & Execution Protocol**

To prevent front-end code complexity and context bloat, the implementation is broken down into discrete **Runs**.
* **The LOG.md Protocol:** At the end of each run, the active agent must write a summary to a `LOG.md` file detailing what was accomplished, any deviations from the plan, and instructions for the next agent.
* **Run Initialization:** The next run must always start by reading `Implementation Plan.md` and `LOG.md`.

**The Service Layer Separation:**
The implementation will separate physics computation, global state, visual representations, and WebGL graphics into isolated modules.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  
│ PHASE 1: THE MVP (Core Relativistic Mechanics & Flat HUD Layout)        │  
│ └── Math Service \-\> Zustand State \-\> SVG Graphs \-\> Hardcoded Run       │  
└───────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘  
                                    ▼  
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  
│ PHASE 2: PRODUCTION DATA & INTERLOCKS (Coordinate Sourcing & Logic)    │  
│ └── Dual JSON Catalogs \-\> Astrometric Switcher \-\> Sliders & Locks      │  
└───────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘  
                                    ▼  
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  
│ PHASE 3: THE HIGH-FIDELITY SCENE GRAPH (WebGL Visualization Canopy)     │  
│ └── 3D Star Cloud \-\> Trajectory Vectors \-\> Midpoint Flip Indicators    │  
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

## **2\. Run-by-Run Execution Checklist**

### **🏃 Run 1: Standalone Physics Core (src/physics/)**
* **Objective:** Build a self-contained, high-performance relativistic flight simulation decoupled from React.
* **Tasks:**
  * Implement continuous-thrust sub-light kinematic functions integrating relativistic dilation profiles.
  * Implement the superluminal Alcubierre Warp track (instantaneous/linear).
  * Write unit tests using the baseline parameters (Sol to Proxima Centauri).
* **Completion:** Write `LOG.md` summarizing the physics core implementation.

### **🏃 Run 2: Python Data Pipeline (scripts/)**
* **Objective:** Scale the astro-database using real celestial coordinate registries.
* **Tasks:**
  * Build a Python pipeline running astroquery to build locally cached JSON databases (`stars.json`, `systemic.json`).
  * Translate Right Ascension, Declination, and Parallax into 3D Cartesian coordinates relative to Sol.
* **Completion:** Write `LOG.md` detailing the generated data files.

### **🏃 Run 3: Global State & Astrometric Logic (src/state/)**
* **Objective:** Instantiate a centralized global store (Zustand) to handle core flight parameters.
* **Tasks:**
  * Handle flight parameters (Origin, Destination, Mass, Acceleration, Speed Cap, Drive Core).
  * Wire the Astrometric Regime Switcher (Interstellar vs. Systemic).
  * Implement unidirectional flow: Time is an output variable, instantly calculated upon input changes.
* **Completion:** Write `LOG.md` outlining the state store and interlocks.

### **🏃 Run 4: Dashboard Shell & UI Controls (src/components/)**
* **Objective:** Draft the strict 100vh non-scrollable dashboard canvas and static controls.
* **Tasks:**
  * Implement the Global Header acting as a static data snapshot.
  * Implement the Left Column (Flight Config) using standard sliders and dropdowns.
  * Implement the Right Column (Telemetry HUD) using tabular monospace fonts.
* **Completion:** Write `LOG.md` summarizing the UI layout implementation.

### **🏃 Run 5: 2D Analytics Charts (src/components/charts/)**
* **Objective:** Generate reactive SVG paths mapping Velocity vs. Time and Position vs. Time.
* **Tasks:**
  * Render sub-light charts (symmetric triangles/trapezoids and S-Curves) with "Flip & Decel" lines.
  * Render warp profiles showing instantaneous velocity jumps and linear position progression.
* **Completion:** Write `LOG.md` covering the charting modules.

### **🏃 Run 6: High-Fidelity WebGL Scene Graph (src/components/visualizer/)**
* **Objective:** Mount the WebGL 3D universe viewport.
* **Tasks:**
  * Initialize a Three.js / React Three Fiber canvas inside the center column.
  * Inject coordinates from the active JSON manifest to project an interactive 3D point cloud.
  * Draw travel vectors and deceleration horizons (for sub-light).
* **Completion:** Write `LOG.md` finalizing the MVP visualizer.

## **3\. Mathematical Validation & Test Suite**

Use these real-world baseline parameters to verify the mathematical accuracy of the kinematics engine.

### **Test Case A: Sub-light Constant Acceleration (No Speed Cap)**

* **Origin:** Sol ![][image9]  
* **Destination:** Proxima Centauri (Distance Vector: ![][image15])  
* **Thrust Profile:** ![][image16] continuous acceleration (![][image17]) to the spatial midpoint, with matching deceleration to zero at the destination.  
* **Expected Mathematical Output:**  
  * *Onboard Passenger Proper Time (![][image1]):* ![][image18]  
  * *Earth Observer Coordinate Time (![][image3]):* ![][image19]  
  * *Peak Midpoint Velocity (![][image20]):* ![][image21] (95% the speed of light)

### **Test Case B: Superluminal Warp Drive (FTL Metric)**

* **Origin:** Sol ![][image9]  
* **Destination:** Proxima Centauri (Distance Vector: ![][image22])  
* **Propulsion Engine:** Tier 5 Alcubierre Warp Drive  
* **Configured Parameter:** Warp Factor ![][image23] (Ten times the speed of light)  
* **Expected Mathematical Output:**  
  * *Lorentz Factor (![][image5]):* Locked hard to ![][image6]  
  * *Onboard Passenger Proper Time (![][image1]):* ![][image24] (![][image25])  
  * *Earth Observer Coordinate Time (![][image3]):* ![][image24] (![][image25])  
  * *Symmetry Rule:* Clocks on Earth and onboard the ship must remain perfectly synchronized down to the millisecond.
